observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the lacking Bank Reconciliation statements and to record immediately any book reconciling items that require adjustments and corrections in the general ledger pursuant to Section 74 of PD 1445 and Sections 3.2, 3.3 and 3.4 of COA Circular No 96-011; to validate the correctness and existence of the cash in bank balances as of year-end.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Accountant, send demand letters to require all accountable officials and employees concerned to liquidate or refund immediately the overdue cash advances granted to them, otherwise, the necessary sanction for its settlement such as withholding of salary to be applied to the unliquidated cash advances shall be enforced or the penal provision of Section 128 of PD No. 1445 be applied against the accountable officers who failed to settle their accountabilities after the demand.
observations.managementAction: We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure that cash advances are not to be granted to officials and employees with previous cash accountabilities following applicable laws, rules, and regulations and to attach a certification on the grant of new cash advance that previous cash advances of the concerned accountable officers were liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor prospectively record the outright purchases of supplies and materials to its appropriate inventory balances of the accounts in the year-end financial statements. Likewise, maintain the Perpetual Inventory Method of accounting for supplies to establish control in the receipt and disposition of the inventory items. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all unissued or undelivered inventories to end-users or department of the Municipality and prepare the necessary journal entry to recognize the same.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require/instruct the: a. Municipal Accountant to: 1. Request from probable source LGUs of the unliquidated fund transfer/ unremitted RPT Share by the Municipality and/or coordinate with the implementing offices/departments to help identify the source agency/ies so that unremitted share or excess amount can be returned or remitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive and other Municipal Officials concerned agreed to do the following: a. Submit the lacking supporting documents indicated in the attached schedule in order to establish the necessity, validity, propriety and legality of the disbursements made; b. Strictly adhere with the provisions of the Revised IRR of R.A.9184 in all its procurement activity. Alternative methods of procurement shall be resorted to only in highly exceptional cases provided for under R.A. No. 9184. In all instances, the procuring entity shall ensure that the most advantageous price for the Municipality is being obtained; and c. observations.recommendation:
1.10. The audit observation and recommendations were communicated to the
Management through AOM No. 15, dated May 6, 2025.
1.11. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to strictly
comply with the requirement of RA 7160 and the NGAS Manual for
LGUs in the setting-up of RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning
of the year.
1.12. We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant, Acting
Municipal Treasurer, and Municipal Assessor agreed to perform
periodic reconciliation of their balances and records to establish a
reliable and realistic basis for the annual setting-up of tax receivables
and collections efforts.
1.13. We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to
recognize in the books any necessary adjustments upon
reconciliation.

2.15. The audit observation and recommendations were communicated to the
Management through AOM No. 11, dated April 15, 2025.
2.16. We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the:
a. Inventory Committee to conduct an annual physical count of PPE
in a comprehensive manner, prepare the RPCPPE using the
prescribed format, and submit the report to the Audit Team on or
before January 31st of the following year;
b. General Services Office to update and prepare the Property Cards
for each class of PPE using the prescribed format, conduct periodic
reconciliation with the records of the Accounting Office, and effect
the necessary adjustments immediately; and
c. Personnel concerned to consider the one-time cleansing of PPE
accounts, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006, as soon as the
Municipality has corrected the deficiencies noted herein and as soon
as preparation for the said activity is deemed adequate.

3.10. The audit observation and recommendations were communicated to the
Management through AOM No. 18, dated June 3, 2025.
3.11. We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Reclassify the completed projects from Construction in Progress to
appropriate Property, Plant, and Equipment accounts and provide the
corresponding allowance for depreciation in compliance with IPSAS 17;
b. Reconcile with the Municipal Engineer the ₱104.445 million beginning
balance of the Construction in Progress–Infrastructure Assets account;
c. Ensure that Certificates of Completion are attached or submitted
completely to the Accounting Office by the Engineering Office upon
processing of final billing or payments to contractors; and
d. Prepare the corresponding subsidiary ledgers and reconcile the balances
thereof with the Municipal Engineer at the end of the year. The said ledgers
should then be submitted not later than the fourteenth day of February of
every year.

4.12. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. Review the reconciling items, secure appropriate supporting
documents, and cause the immediate recording of the reconciling
items;
b. Consider hiring additional Accounting staff to be assigned for the
preparation of the monthly bank reconciliation statements;
c. Submit the bank reconciliation statement for DBP Current Account
No. 0756-xxxx-30 under the General Fund as soon as it is finalized;
and
d. Analyze and verify discrepancies, errors, and other reconciling
items as they occur and subsequently prepare journal entry
vouchers for the immediate recording of the
corrections/adjustments in the general ledger so as to ensure
accurate presentation of the Cash and Cash Equivalents and other
affected accounts in the financial statements.

5.11. We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
a. Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct periodic physical
inventory of supplies pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS Manual
for LGUs, Volume I;
b. Require the preparation of the Report of the Physical Count of
Inventories (RPCI) and its submission to the Audit Team not later
than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second
semesters, respectively, as well as to the Municipal Accountant for
reconciliation with the accounting records;
c. Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain the perpetual
inventory records of Supplies Ledger Cards (SLC) for each
commodity/stock, and the General Services Office (GSO) Designate
to likewise maintain stock cards and property cards for supplies; and
d. Direct the Municipal Accountant to record the necessary
adjustments for Food Supplies for Distribution account.

6.8. We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the
Municipal Accountant to:
a. Compute for the correct amount of accumulated depreciation of
Road Networks account as of December 31, 2024, and prepare the
necessary adjusting entries;
b. Compute for the depreciation expense for Road Networks without
applying any residual value; and
c. Include prospectively in the Notes to the Financial Statements
disclosures of the Municipality’s Road Network System, as provided
in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-008.
6.9. We also recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the
GSO Designate and the Inventory Committee to prepare the Report on
Local Road Network and Report on the Physical Count of the Road
Network System, respectively.

7.11. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to record the
monetization of leave credits of all personnel, resigned or incumbent,
under Terminal Leave Benefits account.
7.12. We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to take
up the accrual of Leave Benefits Payable upon determination of the cost
or money value of the accumulated leave credits to be monetized for the
year.
7.13. We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to
disclose in the financial statements the details and financial impact of
the impending full and retroactive recognition of the cost or money
value of the accumulated leave credits of all municipal personnel whilst
awaiting clear budgeting guidelines thereon.

8.13. We recommended and the Municipal Budget Officer agreed to
coordinate with the Municipal Accountant in determining the proper
account codes to be used for both budgeting and accounting purposes
based on the nature of the expense items to facilitate consistency and to
avoid the indiscriminate use of Other Maintenance and Operating
Expense account.
8.14. We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to record
accounts and transactions based on their nature and in accordance with
the RCA for LGUs, and not solely rely on the account codes indicated
in Obligation Requests.

9.8. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure
henceforth that the financial statements properly present the current
and non-current portions of the Loans Payable account as at
December 31 of the current year.

10.11. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure
henceforth that the effects of the change in accounting policy are:
a. Properly presented in the comparative financial statements by
adjusting and indicating the adjusted opening balances of the
affected accounts for the earliest period presented therein; and
b. Adequately disclosed by providing details as to the nature, reason,
and the amount of the adjustments for each financial statement line
item in the Notes to Financial Statements.

11.8. We recommended and the Acting Municipal Treasurer agreed to:
a. Ensure that the Collectors/Tellers’ preparation of Report of
Collections and Deposits, and remittance to the Liquidating Officer
are done on a daily basis;
b. Review and verify the Report of Collections and Deposits, and
appropriate attachments submitted by the Collectors, Tellers, and
Liquidating Officer; and
c. Update and balance the Cashbook – Cash in Treasury and
Cashbook – Cash in Bank on a daily basis.
11.9. We also recommended and the Acting Municipal Treasurer and
Municipal Accountant agreed to perform monthly reconciliation of
recorded collections and deposits.
11.10. We further recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to
designate a municipal vehicle for the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to
ensure the safety of government funds and its personnel while in transit.

12.7. We recommended and the Acting Municipal Treasurer agreed to
submit to this Office the signed memorandum of agreement with the
Landbank of the Philippines for the utilization of the Link.Biz Portal.
Henceforth, all contracts and agreements with intermediaries should
contain the Auditability Clause and be submitted to this Office within
five days from approval thereof.
12.8. We also recommended and the Acting Municipal Treasurer, Municipal
Accountant, and the focal person agreed to devise mechanisms to:
a. Streamline the e-Collection processes by adhering strictly to the
provisions of COA Circular No. 2021-014;
b. Ensure that the List of Daily Collections and Report of e-Collections
and Deposits are prepared on a daily basis or whenever there is a
transaction; and
c. Ensure that daily reconciliation of e-Collections and
remittance/deposit is performed by providing the collectors with the
daily transaction reports generated by the intermediary or by
verifying with the LBP weAccess portal. Likewise, the same should
be forwarded to the Accounting in-charge for monthly bank
reconciliation purposes.
12.9. We likewise recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer agreed
to coordinate and discuss with LBP the bond requirement, and require
the posting thereof in favor of the Municipality.

13.9. We recommended and the BAC agreed to ensure henceforth that
Notices of Award are issued, and performance and warranty securities
are posted and submitted for alternative modes of procurements,
specifically Negotiated Procurements for the hiring of highly technical
consultants.
13.10. We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure
henceforth the completeness of supporting documents before payment
or disbursement therefor is processed.

14.6. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to take up the
following in the books of accounts:
Account Name Debit (₱) Credit (₱)
Prior Period Adjustment 8,000.00
Due to BIR 8,000.00

15.17. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the
adjustments to correct the total balance of Government Equity, Due to
Other Funds, Due from Other Funds, and Trust Liability-DRRMF
accounts.
15.18. We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to
facilitate the complete and actual cash transfer of the
unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE
to the Special Trust Fund in the Trust Fund books as soon as possible,
and ensure that the appropriate accounts are used in accounting
therefor.

16.8. We recommended and the Municipal Mayor, the Sangguniang Bayan,
and other concerned municipal heads and personnel agreed to
prioritize the finalization of the updating of the Municipality’s local
revenue code, pursuant to Section 191 of R.A. No. 7160.

17.13. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to set up and
maintain a special account for the municipal economic enterprises,
complete with subsidiary records and prepare separate sets of trial
balance and financial statements.

18.11. We recommended and the General Services Office agreed to maintain
a repair and maintenance record/history of each equipment/vehicle
owned and/or operated by the Municipality to avoid unnecessary repair
and maintenance expenses. We suggest that a repair history form be
devised, indicating the type of repair undertaken, the date when it was
made, and other pertinent data for the information and guidance of the
COA, the agency, and others concerned.
18.12. We also recommended and the Accounting Office agreed to require the
submission of the said repair history as supporting document for
disbursements on repairs and maintenance of machineries, equipment,
and motor vehicles.

19.10. We recommended and the General Services Office agreed to require
the concerned drivers to accomplish the Driver’s Trip Tickets properly
and completely by indicating all the details required by the form.
19.11. We also recommended and the General Services Office agreed to revise
and improve the format of the Monthly Fuel Consumption report by
including the details provided in the Manual on Audit for Fuel
Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles, which are necessary in
assessing the reasonableness of periodic fuel consumption of the
Municipality.
19.12. We further recommended and the GSO Designate agreed to consider
the procurement of aftermarket odometers as supplement or
replacement for the defective odometers if such defective ones are
proven to be beyond repair, and repairing and replacing such are
expected to be much more costly.
19.13. We likewise recommended and the Accounting Office agreed to require
the submission of properly accomplished Driver’s Trip Tickets and
Monthly Fuel Consumption Reports as supporting documents for
disbursements on Fuel, Oil, and Lubricants Expenses of the
Municipality.

observations.recommendation: Refer to respective earlier recommendations in prior years' AARs.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the General Services Officer to submit the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We also recommended and the General Services Officer agreed to prepare Property Cards and assign a Property Number and Property Sticker for each PPE item. 




We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the PPELCs in addition to the Subsidiary Ledgers maintained and to reconcile the postings/records of their PPE thereon.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct all accountable officials and employees concerned to comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and liquidate or refund immediately the cash advances granted to them, otherwise the necessary sanction for its settlement such as withholding of salary to be applied to the unliquidated cash advances shall be enforced or the penal provisions of Section 128 of PD No. 1445 be applied.

We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to see to it that cash advances should not be granted to officials and employees with previous cash accountabilities in accordance with applicable laws, rules and regulations and to attach a certification on the grant of a new cash advance that previous cash advances of the concerned accountable officers were liquidated.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the persons responsible to: 
a.	Submit all due DVs, LRs and supporting documents immediately for verification and post audit;



b.	Consider assigning additional personnel to the Municipal Accounting Office to facilitate prompt submission of the above-mentioned documents; and

c.	Strictly enforce the submission of paid DVs within ten (10) days after the end of the month. Moreover, closely monitor the submission of the reports and supporting documents by the accountable officers for immediate recording in the books of accounts so as to facilitate submission to the Auditor within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the GSO and other members of the Inventory Committee to formulate a system or flow of procedures for the receipt, issuance, documentation, recording, and inventory taking of in-kind donations, for guidance and uniform application.

We also recommended and the Municipal Health Officer and other municipal employees concerned agreed to submit relevant documents/ reports to the GSO upon receipt of the donations-in-kind so that inspection of items received can be made.

We further recommended and the GSO, MHO and other municipal employees concerned agreed to conduct regular monitoring of donated items and accomplish the Requisition Issue Slip (RIS) and/or Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) and submit relative report to the Municipal Accountant so that remaining balances, if any, shall be costed and recorded in the books or in the registries.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require/instruct the:

a.	Concerned implementing offices/ departments to implement immediately the various PPAs funded by other LGUs;

b.	Municipal Accountant to:

b.1. Exert extra effort to locate the documents supporting the non-moving balances, ascertain the reasons why the same remained in the books for a long period of time and make the necessary adjusting entries in case of errors in recording; and 

b.2. Request from probable source LGUs of the unliquidated fund transfer/ unremitted RPT Share by the Municipality and/or coordinate with the implementing offices/ departments to help identify the source agency/ies so that unremitted share or excess amount can be returned or remitted;

c.	Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer to immediately return or remit the excess amount for those completed projects with the identified source LGU; and

d.	Acting Municipal Treasurer to see to it that the shares of the Barangays and the Province from RPT collections are released within the prescribed period in order that the funds can immediately be used for the benefit of their constituents.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to enforce collections of unpaid market rental fee plus interest thereof, otherwise impose penal provisions as provided in Municipal Ordinance No. 2011-01.

We further recommended and Management agreed to prepare a database or back up files to serve as reference and continuously monitor the payments made in case of loss of the Individual Index Card.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the concerned personnel to perform the following:

a. 	Prepare an Organizational Structure and Staffing Pattern Plan, and formulate policies and guidelines so that the necessity and propriety of hiring job order employees are justified;

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to see to it that all the necessary supporting documents required for payment of various expenses are attached to disbursement vouchers before effecting payment pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001, dated June 14, 2012.

Management should continually evaluate the utilization of the funds intended for development projects through the use of these financial statements.

   We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant:

   a. To prepare and submit  the  bank  reconciliation  statements  for  the
      period stated above; and to record immediately any unbook  reconciling

      pursuant to Section 74 of PD 1445 and Sections 3.2, 3.3 and 3.4 of COA
      Circular No. 96-011 to prove the correctness and existence of the cash
      in  bank balances as of year-end.


   b. To make  representation  with  the  bank  management  concerning  bank
      reconciling items.

   The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to  the  management
   thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2020-01 dated February 3, 2020.

Recommendations


   We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an inventory committee  to
   conduct physical count of all the property, plant and equipment owned  by
   the Municipality and prepare and submit the Report on the Physical  Count

   We  likewise  recommended  that  the  Accounting  Office   maintain   the
   prescribed PPELC which should be reconciled with the RPCPPE to  establish
   the correctness of the PPE accounts amounting to P91.009 million.


   The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management thru
   Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2020-05 dated February 20, 2020.

Recommendations


   We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the  Municipal  Accountant
to:


     a. Effect prior years’ adjustment on depreciation  equivalent  to  the
        amount that  should  have  been  provided  from  the  date  of  its
        acquisition/construction;


     b. Provide allowance for depreciation on depreciable assets regularly;
        and

     c. Record  immediately  the  depreciation  expense  in  the  books  of
        accounts in order to present fairly  the  value  of  the  Property,
        Plant and Equipment in the financial statements.


   The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management  thru
   Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2020-06 dated February 20, 2020.

Recommendations


   We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:


   a. Strictly enforce the settlement of unliquidated cash  advances  as  of
      December 31, 2019 and thereafter;


   b. Stop granting additional cash advance to officers and employees unless
      the previous one has been fully liquidated to  avoid  accumulation  of
      the same;


   c. Initiate appropriate actions against erring officials  and  employees,
      if warranted;


   d. Require the Municipal Accountant  to  send  follow-up  written  demand
      letter to officers/employees with accountability  that  is  no  longer
      connected with the Municipality.


   The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to  the  management
   thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2020-02 dated February 3, 2020.

Recommendation


   We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant
   and  Municipal  Budget  Officer  to   stop   the   practice   of   paying
   government/employer’s contributions in excess of P100.00  per  month  per
   employee as provided in Section 2,  Rule  IV  of  E.O No. 90, DBM & COA Joint
   Circular No. 93-2 and Republic Act 9676.


   We also recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan and the  Municipal  Budget
   Officer revisit the existing rules and  regulations  pertaining  to  HDMF
   contribution-employer’s  share  for  guidance   in   the   approval   and
   preparation of the annual municipal budget.


   The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to  the  management
   thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2020-07 dated February 20, 2020.

Recommendations


   We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the  Municipal  Accountant
   and Municipal Treasurer to remit immediately the amount of P351,350.13 to
   the Bureau of Internal Revenue.


   We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the recording or
   journal entry of Due to  BIR-Trust  Fund  and  make  necessary  adjusting
   entry/ies, if any.


   Henceforth, taxes withheld from payments to  suppliers  and  salaries  of
   employees be remitted promptly  and  regularly  in  accordance  with  the
   provisions of BIR Revenue Regulation No. 16-2005.


   The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to  the  management
   thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2020-08 dated February 21, 2020.

Recommendations


   We recommended that the  Municipal  Mayor  together  with  the  Municipal
   Engineer and Municipal Planning  and  Development  Officer  maximize  the
   utilization of the 20% Development Fund allocated for the  implementation
   of various projects, programs and activities that are envisioned to  help
   in the attainment of social, economic and  environmental  development  so
   that  optimum  benefits  derived  therefrom  can  be   availed   by   the
   constituents.


   We   also   recommended   that   the   Municipal   Development    Council
   provide/identify specific project/activity on every  programmed  projects
   so as to ensure its implementation.


   The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to  the  management
   thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2020-04 dated February 10, 2020.

Recommendations


   We recommended that the Municipality ensure  the  implementation  of  all
   PPAs identified under the Local Disaster Risk Reduction  Management  Fund
   Investment  Plan  (LDRRMFIP)  and  Budget.   Likewise,   prioritize   the
   acquisition  of  disaster  preparedness  equipment   and   infrastructure
   projects to calamity-stricken area of the constituents.


   We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the  Municipal
   Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (MDRRMC) to ensure that plans,
   programs and activities in the LDRRMFIP are feasible and conduct  regular
   monitoring on the implementation of Projects, Plans and Activities in the
   plan of the Municipality.


   The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to  the  management
   thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2020-03 dated January 20, 2020.

Recommendations


   We strongly recommended that the  Municipal  Mayor  require/instruct  the
   Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to  submit  immediately  the
   unsubmitted disbursement vouchers and cancelled checks for post-audit.

   We also recommended that internal control  be  strengthened  by  ensuring
   that the Municipality strictly complies with the afore-cited implementing
   laws, rules and regulations.
   The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management  thru
   Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2020-09 dated February 21, 2020.

   We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, being the Chairman of the Board:


   a. Direct the  Local  School  Board  to  maximize  the  use  of  the  budgeted
      allocation  of  SEF  for  the  implementation  of  priority  projects,

      using SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1 (Annex B) as prescribed  in
      the same Joint Circular.

We also recommended that  the  Municipal  Mayor  instruct  the  Municipal Treasurer or the Payroll Master to effect deductions due to absences from the RATA of the municipal officials concerned.

Further, Notice of Disallowance will be issued for the excess  RATA  paid to  some members  of  the  Sangguniang  Bayan  in  the  total  amount  of P66,000.00 for failure to  have  100%  attendance  in  the  regular   and special sessions.

The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management  thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2017-13 dated February 17, 2017.

Recommendations

We recommended that management refrain from granting additional  benefits and allowances to Job Hires.

We also recommended that persons determined to be liable cause the refund of the CY 2016 Clothing Allowance, Mid/Year-End Bonus and  Cash  Gift  at P23,000.00 per LnB clerk, or a  total  of  P138,000.00  which  were  paid contrary to Item 4.0 of DBM Budget Circular No. 2012-1 dated February 23, 2012 and Item 3.0 of DBM Budget Circular No. 2016-3 dated April 28, 2016.

The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management  thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2016-25 dated December 28, 2016.


Management Comment

The Municipal Treasurer promised that effective February 2017, deductions will be effected from the payroll of the concerned job hire on the amount disallowed.

Recommendation

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  submit  the   required supporting documents such as proof that savings  were  generated  through cost cutting measures undertaken by LGU to prove validity of the claim.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management  thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2017-16 dated February 17, 2017.

Recommendation

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal  Treasurer  to  submit  the  above-mentioned  documents  to facilitate further review of the concerned transactions.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management  thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2017-21 dated February 17, 2017.

Recommendation

We recommended that the Municipality adhere strictly to the provisions of RA No. 9679 and to (a) limit the payment of the employer’s share  to  the HDMF Fund to the rate authorized; and (b) require the employees to refund to the Municipality the excess employer’s share contribution remitted  to the Fund in their behalf.

The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management  thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2017-23 dated February 27, 2017.

Recommendations

We recommended that the Municipal  Accountant make   sure  that   payment of monetization of leave credits shall be made after  submission  of  the required approved application for leave and  certification  of  available leave credits pursuant to CSC Omnibus Rules on Leave Implementing Book  V of Executive Order No. 292 (The Administrative Code of  1987),  with  the request of the employee duly approved by the agency head subject  to the availability of funds for the monetization of 50% or more of  accumulated leave credits.

We further recommended that  the  designated  Human  Resource  Management Officer submit the lacking supporting documents required for monetization of leave credits.


The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management  thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2017-08 dated February 10, 2017.

Recommendation

We   recommended that   the   Municipal   Mayor direct   the  Bids    and Awards Committee to instruct the BAC Secretariat to post all  procurement in the Portal as  prescribed  in  the  above-mentioned  laws,  rules  and regulations  to  promote  transparency  and  achieve  efficiency  in  the procurement process of the municipality.

The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management  thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2017-03 dated January 5, 2017.


Management Comment

The BAC Chairman commented  that  access to  PhilGEPS  website  is   very slow thus it is time consuming  to  publish  all  procurements,   but  he promised to post Invitation for Price Quotation in 3  conspicuous  places within the Municipality for purchases below P200,000.00.

Recommendation

We strongly recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct  the  Municipal Accountant and  Municipal  Treasurer  to  immediately  transfer  barangay shares from  taxes to facilitate implementations of barangay PPAs.

The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management  thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2017-18 dated February 17, 2017.

Recommendations

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant submit to the  Office  of  the Auditor  the  lacking  disbursement  vouchers  and  supporting  documents enumerated in Annex F.

We also recommend that internal control be strengthened by ensuring  that the  OIC-Municipal  Accountant  strictly  comply  with  the   afore-cited implementing laws, rules and regulations.

The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management  thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2016-26 dated December 28, 2016.

Recommendation

We recommended  that  management  strictly  observe  the  above-mentioned provision on the proper utilization of SEF to facilitate the availability of funds in the implementation of PPAs relative thereto.

The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to  the  management thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2017-22 dated February 17, 2017.

Recommendations

We strongly recommended  that  management  observe  properly  procurement process in accordance with RA 9184.

Likewise, we recommended that the municipality shall draw check  payments directly in  the  name  of  the  supplier  in  conformity  with  existing regulations and withheld the appropriate taxes due the government.

The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management  thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2017-04 dated January 5, 2017.

Recommendation

We  strongly  recommended  adherence  to  the   above-cited   rules   and regulations to prevent imposition of proper sanctions relative thereto.

Notice of Disallowance will be issued on the disbursements of  government funds amounting to P68,557.00.

The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management  thru Audit Observation Memoranda No. 2017-05 dated January 25, 2017and 2017-14 dated February 17, 2017.

Recommendation

We recommended that the BAC Chairman and the Municipal Accountant see  to  it  that  all  required/necessary  bidding  documents  are  attached   to disbursement vouchers for  payment  of  purchases  or  contracts  through public bidding pursuant to Section 9.1  of  COA  Circular  No.  2012-001, dated June 14, 2012 and Rule XI of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184.

The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management  thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2017-10 dated February 17, 2017.

Recommendation

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned  department heads  to  submit  the  duly  accomplished  AAPSI on  each  finding  and recommendations of their concern, as presented in Annex N, and  discussed in detail in Part III-Detailed Observations and  Recommendations  portion  of the CY 2015 Management Letter, in compliance with Section  93  of  the General Provisions of RA  No.  10651,  otherwise  known  as  the  General Appropriations Act of 2015.

The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management  thru AOM No. 2017-01 dated January 3, 2017.

Recommendation

We recommended that  the  Municipal  Accountant   prepare  Journal  Entry Voucher (JEV) in all  financial  transactions  of  the  Municipality  and attach the same to disbursement vouchers and journals pursuant to Section 31 of the New Government Accounting System for  Local  Government  Units, Volume II.

The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management  thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2017-12 dated February 17, 2017.


Management Comment

The Municipal Accountant commented that JEVs were prepared but  were  not printed-out and attached to disbursement vouchers and reports.

Recommendation

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the employees   concerned to submit to the Auditor a copy of all contracts, purchase orders and the like,  including  all  supporting  documents  forming  part  thereof   by reference  or  incorporation  within  five  (5)  working  days  from  the execution of the contracts and issuance of purchase  orders  pursuant  to COA Circular No. 2009-001.

The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management  thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2017-11 dated February 17, 2017.
